value.description: Run a sequential 10-run supervised BookSeller campaign with messy prompts, project-scoped memory/context checks, disposable workspace apply, fail-closed unsafe patching, and a compact Codex-readable campaign report.
Keep BookSeller architecture memory retrieval green and project-scoped.
Keep BookSeller architecture memory retrieval green and project-scoped.
Prove BookSeller builder preview is project-scoped, includes BookSeller source context, and performs no file writes before approval.
Start with a vague persistence request, let IronDev ask for detail, then answer with storage facts and require a saved discussion document route.
Prove BookSeller patch application is restricted to an explicit disposable workspace with hashes, build/test evidence, comparison review, and no real repo writes.
Prove BookSeller disposable workspace apply fails closed when a proposal attempts to write outside the explicit disposable workspace.
Prove CriticAgent can review a Codex-facing failure package generated from a failed BookSeller batch.
Keep CriticAgent review of BookSeller failure evidence green.
Keep CriticAgent review of BookSeller failure evidence green.
Prove a BookSeller discussion can become a real SQL ProjectDocument with an exact source discussion link and semantic trace evidence.
Prove a BookSeller project document can generate multiple SQL ProjectTickets and each ticket preserves the exact ProjectDocumentVersion source link.
Prove a failed BookSeller batch produces a compact Codex-readable failure package with evidence paths, repro command, validation command, and recommended next action.
Keep the BookSeller failed-batch Codex handoff path green.
Keep BookSeller failed batch handoff green.
Keep BookSeller failed batch handoff green.
Prove a failed BookSeller Test Agent run is packaged into Codex-readable JSON and Markdown evidence.
Intentionally fail a BookSeller batch after one passing memory search so Codex handoff can prove evidence from a real multi-step failed batch.
Intentionally fail a BookSeller memory search assertion so the Codex failure package can prove BookSeller-scoped evidence handoff.
Prove messy BookSeller prompts route by behaviour, not exact wording, across vague storage, typo-heavy ticket creation, shorthand discussion saving, and build-preview safety.
Keep the BookSeller messy prompt batch green through TesterAgent.
Keep the BookSeller messy prompt batch green through TesterAgent.
Prove PlannerAgent can draft a BookSeller-scoped plan from a vague goal without executing or patching.
Prove PlannerAgent drafts from vague BookSeller intent without executing or patching.
Prove PlannerAgent drafts from vague BookSeller intent without executing or patching.
Prove BookSeller exists as a project-scoped dogfood fixture with accepted memory, a simple ticket fixture, and a CLI/Test Agent plan that reports project = BookSeller without IronDev memory bleed.
Prove BookSeller memory search remains project-scoped and authority-ranked when queries contain tempting IronDev/Codex/self-improvement language.
Keep the BookSeller retrieval chaos batch green through TesterAgent.
Keep the BookSeller retrieval chaos batch green through TesterAgent.
Prove RetrieverAgent can package BookSeller accepted memory without IronDev bleed.
Prove RetrieverAgent packages BookSeller accepted memory without IronDev bleed.
Exercise a vague BookSeller storage request, answer IronDev's clarification, then verify replay stays green.
Prove SupervisorAgent can coordinate BookSeller memory plus builder preview while preserving no-write boundaries.
Prove SupervisorAgent coordinates BookSeller memory plus builder preview while preserving no-write boundaries.
Prove SupervisorAgent can coordinate BookSeller memory retrieval plus BookSeller builder preview validation without writes.
Prove SupervisorAgent can coordinate BookSeller memory plus the BookSeller project smoke.
Prove SupervisorAgent coordinates BookSeller memory plus the BookSeller project smoke.
Prove the Test Agent can run a BookSeller-scoped validation step after builder preview without applying code.
Prove TesterAgent can run the BookSeller project-scoped fixture plan and return a structured report for project = BookSeller.
Prove a BookSeller project ticket created from a SQL-backed project document preserves and resolves the exact ProjectDocumentVersion source link.
Prove a BookSeller ticket with SourceDocumentVersionId assembles builder context with the linked source document memory, without generating code or applying patches.
Prove a vague BookSeller persistence prompt asks for more detail first, then routes the clarified answer into a dry-run discussion document action.
Prove IronDev has OpenAI-only model profiles, eight registered agent stubs, and one working TesterAgent path that can execute an existing Test Agent plan.
Prove builder proposal generation remains proposal-first and does not write files before approval.
Run the deterministic Alpha code standards gate so Codex can see build/test/format/audit status plus code-shape warnings before adding more memory-spine ribs.
Prove Codex can query IronDev dogfood project memory through a Weaviate-backed CLI search and receive compact source-linked JSON before editing.
Prove named-memory queries promote the exact accepted document above broad umbrella architecture.
Prove named-memory queries promote exact accepted batch documents across current and previous batches.
Prove CriticAgent can review a Codex-facing failure package and return an actionable evidence review.
Prove CriticAgent can review an IronDev failure package and return an actionable evidence-backed recommendation.
Keep CriticAgent review of IronDev failure evidence green.
Keep the IronDev failure package handoff path green.
Keep the IronDev failure package handoff path green.
Prove failed Test Agent reports can be packaged into Codex-readable JSON and Markdown with expected/actual evidence, likely areas, repro commands, validation commands, and safety rules.
Intentionally fail a memory search assertion so the failure package handoff can prove Test Agent failures are packaged for Codex.
Prove builder context assembly includes the ticket's linked SQL ProjectDocumentVersion without generating code or applying patches.
Prove real Weaviate retrieval can surface a stronger cross-project candidate while IronDev project filtering rejects it and accepts the active IronDev project memory as primary authority.
Prove the first slice of IronDev's memory spine: Weaviate health plus current IronDev dogfood knowledge retrieval with authority-aware expectations.
Prove a SQL-backed ProjectDocumentVersion can be indexed into semantic memory tables and retrieved as the current authoritative version over an older stale version.
Prove tickets created from SQL-backed project documents preserve and resolve the exact ProjectDocumentVersion source link.
Prove repeated memory reindexing keeps current accepted IronDev memory fresh, stale evidence visible, duplicate counts zero, and BookSeller wrong-project candidates rejected.

Prove SQL-backed ProjectDocumentVersion chunks can be written to Weaviate, returned by a real nearVector query, and corrected by authority/current/stale ranking when raw vector retrieval prefers the stale version.
Prove PlannerAgent turns a bounded vague goal into valid draft Test Agent plan JSON without executing or patching code.
Prove PlannerAgent drafts an IronDev-scoped memory-first plan from a vague self-improvement goal.
Prove PlannerAgent drafts an IronDev-scoped memory-first plan for the larger reliability loop.
Prove PlannerAgent drafts an IronDev-scoped memory-first plan for the 074-093 reliability loop.
Keep QualityAgent deterministic quality gate green inside the 054-073 batch.
Keep the QualityAgent deterministic gate green inside the 074-093 batch.
Prove QualityAgent wraps the deterministic code standards/toolchain gate and returns a structured quality report.
Prove RetrieverAgent returns a richer context bundle with source guidance while preserving the real memory search path.
Prove RetrieverAgent wraps the real memory search path and returns a project-scoped context package with source and ranking evidence.
Prove RetrieverAgent returns the accepted 044-053 batch goal pack as current IronDev context with guidance and trace evidence.
Prove RetrieverAgent returns the accepted 054-073 batch goal pack as current IronDev context.
Prove RetrieverAgent returns the accepted 074-093 batch goal pack as current IronDev context.
Prove the accepted IronDev self-improvement system memory retrieves as the current source for operating rules and next build order.
Prove the 044-053 goal pack is available through Codex-facing IronDev memory search.
Prove the 054-073 goal pack is available through Codex-facing IronDev memory search.
Prove the 074-093 self-improvement goal pack is accepted IronDev memory.
Prove SupervisorAgent can gather project memory, dispatch TesterAgent, and return a compact Codex-facing handoff without changing builder behaviour.
Prove SupervisorAgent retrieves current 054-073 memory and dispatches TesterAgent against a bounded memory smoke.
Prove SupervisorAgent retrieves current 074-093 memory and dispatches TesterAgent against a bounded memory smoke.
Prove SupervisorAgent retrieves current IronDev batch memory and dispatches TesterAgent against a bounded memory smoke plan.
Prove SupervisorAgent selects from a tiny allowed decision set after RetrieverAgent and TesterAgent complete.
Prove TesterAgent can execute the real cross-project Weaviate memory smoke and return a structured report showing IronDev rejects BookSeller memory as primary authority.
Exercise real deterministic Test Agent tools for build, focused tests, formatting, audit, and report schema shape.
Run the key BookSeller and self-improvement proof plans through TesterAgent as one compact 020-030 regression pack.
Run the branch-level PR-ready quality gate for the 022-032 self-improvement batch.
Run the key 034-041 operational self-improvement proofs through TesterAgent as one compact regression pack. Goal 033 is the branch goal document boundary.

Run prior PR gates plus the new 054-070 pack so Codex can see continuity across batches.
Run the 033-043, 044-053, 054-073, and 074-090 proof packs as a compact cross-batch confidence gate.
